name: safe_agentic_workflow
description: Autonomous coding pipeline. Run for any request to write or change code: derives requirements, understands the codebase, reads docs, plans, GENERATES a per-task coding workflow, runs it, validates with the project's tests, and loops through an acceptance gate until the solution passes or the max loop count is reached, then documents the result.

variables.description: The feature/change the user asked for, as originally stated.
The specific, testable definition of done derived from the task.
What the relevant existing code does: files, modules, patterns, and conventions the change must fit.
Relevant facts from project docs (AGENTS.md, README, design notes): how to build/test, constraints, and conventions.
The implementation plan: the relevant areas to change and the ordered coding steps, one entry per area.
A short snake_case identifier derived from the task, used as the generated coding workflow's name/filename (e.g. add_dark_mode).
The name of the generated per-task coding workflow: task_slug followed by _workflow.
How many validation/gate cycles have run so far.
The maximum number of validation/gate cycles allowed before the pipeline stops looping (default 3).
Whether the project's test command exited successfully on the latest implementation.
The captured stdout/stderr and exit status of the latest test run.
Whether the acceptance gate judged the implementation complete and correct against the acceptance criteria.
When the gate rejects: the specific defects/gaps the next coding pass must fix. Empty when accepted.

settings.action: Read task_description. Set acceptance_criteria to a specific, testable definition of done (formalize implied criteria; add no scope). Set task_slug to a short snake_case id (e.g. 'add a dark-mode toggle' -> add_dark_mode). Set coding_workflow_name = task_slug + '_workflow'. Set max_loops=3, gate_iteration=0.
Explore the touched areas using read/search/list tools (never guess). Set codebase_notes: relevant files/modules, patterns/conventions to follow, integration points.
Read AGENTS.md, README, and any design/architecture notes. Set doc_notes: build and TEST commands (the exact test command) and any implementation constraints/conventions.
From acceptance_criteria, codebase_notes, doc_notes, set plan: the AREAS to change and, per area, the ordered coding steps. Ground in the actual files found.

Per the 'botcircuits-coding-workflow-authoring' skill, write intent-only source for a workflow that STARTS with a check_revision branch (targeted fix pass when revision_findings non-empty), then one coding step per plan area (dynamic count), then a final self-check step; NO question steps, NO review/approval steps. Run `botcircuits workflow generate --from <file> --name <coding_workflow_name> --subdir coding --build`. Confirm .botcircuits/workflows/.build/coding/<coding_workflow_name>.json exists.
shell_exec `botcircuits workflow run --name <coding_workflow_name> --initial-args '{"revision_findings": "<current revision_findings, empty on first pass>"}'`. Report what it changed.
shell_exec the doc_notes test command; capture full output and exit status into validation_output. Set tests_passed=true only if exit 0.

Increment gate_iteration by 1. Judge against acceptance_criteria using tests_passed and validation_output. If tests_passed AND criteria fully met: set solution_accepted=true, clear revision_findings. Else set solution_accepted=false and set revision_findings to the specific defects, failing tests, and unmet criteria for the next pass.

Write an implementation summary: what changed, which acceptance_criteria were met/unmet, the final test result from validation_output, and known limitations/follow-ups. If max_loops was hit without acceptance, state so and list outstanding revision_findings.
